mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations down and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects supply-side bottlenecks and fading manufacturing momentum, triggering short-term profit warnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 3.5% forces market participants to price in imminent monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by over 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains elevated, signals unsustainable consumption debt and impending demand collapse, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern yields a positive rank IC for 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the industrial production index during a post-recession recovery phase confirms manufacturing sector normalization and rising capacity utilization, which boosts near-term earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production accelerations during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in over 55% of cases.
